title: Senior Engineer - ML Systems (AI Products)
content:
# Senior Engineer - ML Systems (AI Products)

* Sydney / Australia
* Hybrid
* Data, AI & Machine Learning
* R0017838

## The Role

**The role**

You will lead the design and implementation of large-scale, production-grade distributed systems that power AI features for millions of daily users. You'll own the architecture decisions that keep our systems flexible, cost-effective, and robust, direct strategy for distributed systems, and manage technical debt across the AI Products estate.

Beyond architecture, your impact lies in lifting the technical capability of the entire AI Products team. You will champion engineering excellence, mentor junior engineers, and collaborate across Xero to enhance data usability - applying modern AI research, including Large Language Models, only once it can be engineered into reliable, production-grade systems.

**The team**

You will join the AI Products group, a diverse team of scientists, engineers, product managers, and analysts within our broader Data & Science division. As a Machine Learning Engineer, you'll partner closely with Applied Scientists to build the interfaces and harnesses that safely and reliably transition models from research into production. Together, this collaborative team reduces toil and delivers beautiful, data-driven insights for small businesses.

**The team is currently working on**

* Designing and building highly scalable, distributed production infrastructure to support generative AI features
* Harnessing tools like Python, SQL, and distributed processing engines such as Spark or Dask to handle web-scale data workload.
* Deploying to production environments running on AWS and Kubernetes Integrating modern Large Language Model technologies into product features once they're production-ready

**Where and how you can work**

Xero offers a flexible hybrid working model designed to blend the collaboration of office life with the autonomy of remote work. You will have access to our modern office spaces, with expectations around office days and collaborative 'boost days' aligned to help your team connect and ship great code effectively.

**Here are some of the things we are looking for**

* 5+ years building and operating production Python (or equivalent language) services at scale - this is a software engineering role first; strong system design and coding proficiency are non-negotiable
* A track record of owning services or pipelines in production, including operational/on-call responsibility, incident response, and managing technical debt over time
* Deep understanding of distributed processing principles (Spark, Dask, or similar) alongside strong SQL capabilities
* Demonstrated experience integrating ML models or LLM-based features into production systems - you don't need a research background, but you should be comfortable working alongside Applied Scientists to productionize their work
* Familiarity with ML tooling such as MLFlow, TensorFlow, or PyTorch, and data orchestration tools l

title: Principal Engineer (API Platform)
content:
# Principal Engineer (API Platform)

* Vancouver / Canada
* Hybrid
* Engineering
* R0017976

## The Role

### **The role and its impact**

This is a senior individual contributor and technical leadership role at the heart of one of Xero’s most strategically important initiatives.

As Principal Engineer for Platform API, you will define and drive the long-term technical vision for how Xero exposes its accounting capabilities to external partners (building on our platform) and to internal product teams who depend on a stable, well-designed API layer to move fast.

The decisions you make here will shape how millions of small businesses and their advisors experience Xero, not just through our own products, but through the ecosystem of applications and agents built around them.

Beyond the external surface, this role sits at the intersection of platform engineering and AI. The APIs you design and steward will serve as the trusted foundation for AI- and agent-driven automation across Xero’s accounting workflows; meaning you will be thinking not just about today’s developer experience, but about what it means for an LLM or autonomous agent to safely read, write, and orchestrate complex financial workflows. It is a rare opportunity to do genuinely consequential technical work at scale.

### **The team and how they connect**

The Product Platform team is responsible for the core capabilities that other Xero teams build on; the APIs, SDKs, and platform services that connect Xero’s accounting engine to the world.

We work across domain and organisational boundaries, partnering closely with product management, AI/ML teams, developer relations, and engineering squads across the company to ensure our platform is coherent, reliable, and a genuine accelerator for everyone who builds on it.

As Principal Engineer, you will be a key voice in shaping technical direction not just within the team, but across Xero’s broader engineering community.

### **The team is currently working on**

* Our current technical stack includes an open source graph technologies powered by gRPC, and backend services built in modern languages like C#, Go, or Java.
* We are standing up a developer platform to provide a seamless path from domain models to running APIs integrated with CI/CD and production runtimes.
* You will help deliver across core accounting domains using the Supergraph model to consolidate our technical stack and developer experience.
* The team is beginning a legacy burndown to replace or front expensive back-channel endpoints with coherent, strongly-typed interfaces and stable contracts.

### **Where and how you can work**

This role is based in our Vancouver office. We embrace a hybrid working model that balances the flexibility of remote work with purposeful in-person collaboration.

### **Here are some of the things we are looking for**

* A deep background in backend or platform engineering at scale; ideally in multi-tenant SaaS or distributed systems environments

### About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth, 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

The Product team shapes the experiences that power Revolut. They turn complex problems into simple, scalable solutions — collaborating across Design, Engineering, and Data to build features our customers love.

We’re looking for a Lead Applied AI Engineer who can manage their product roadmap, including day-to-day operations, project execution, and risk assessment.

From conception to launch and beyond, you’ll collaborate cross-functionally and communicate with senior management to be the go-to person for your product.

* Setting your team's goals, success metrics, and roadmap to align with company goals
* Delivering measurable results for sophisticated products under ambiguous situations in a fast-paced environment

### What you'll need

* Experience developing and launching AI products/features from scratch
* High analytical and technical acumen, along with solid business sense and problem-solving skills
* Experience in a data science or machine learning role
* Experience owning machine learning projects, from design to implementation and iteration
* Great Python, SQL, or other programming language skills
* Hands-on experience with deep learning libs (TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, etc.)
* Knowledge of version control, model deployment, and developer environments
* Evidence of impressive mathematical and statistical knowledge
* An entrepreneurial drive to never settle

title: Tallinn | Jobs
content:
# Tallinn

Welcome to Tallinn, where Pipedrive was born.

Come and join Pipedrive’s Tallinn team!

See open positions

## About us

Pipedrive was born in Tallinn. What started as a small startup in a shared office has grown into an international company with an entire building in Tallinn’s vibrant WoHo quarter. Today, Tallinn is home to our engineering and product hub, where 300+ team members from 30+ nationalities work together – many of them engineers.

Pipedrive is proud to be Estonia’s fifth unicorn and has consistently been recognized as one of the best places in the country to work since 2016, according to both the media and our employees. And we’re not just about jobs – at Pipedrive, you’ll have the chance to grow, learn and make a real impact by helping shape a product used by more than 100,000 companies worldwide.

## Our traditions

At Pipedrive, we love to celebrate the moments that matter – from birthdays and work anniversaries to promotions. One of our most beloved traditions is getting together for *Kringle*, a classic Estonian pastry, though you’ll also see pizza, donuts or pancakes make an appearance. To make sure no celebration goes unnoticed, we’ve got a kitchen-alert Slack channel to keep everyone in the loop.

Our biggest annual event is the Summer Garage – a full-day celebration that brings teams together to connect, unwind and enjoy live music performed by our very own band (and sometimes a guest artist or two).

We also host occasional hackathons and innovation accelerators to spark new ideas for improving the product and how we work together.

We believe in a balance of fun and focus. From Lightning Talks where anyone can practice their presentation skills with a topic they care about, to Sudoku tournaments and gaming challenges organized by teammates, there’s always something to get involved in.

We’re also proud to contribute to the wider community: Our team actively supports the local engineering, product and HR scenes in Estonia by hosting in-house meetups, participating in conferences and speaking at events.

Staying active outside of work is also a big part of our culture. Whether it's a friendly game of table tennis, a disc golf match or a padel session – one of the most popular sports among our Tallinn team – there's always fun going on.

## Where you’ll find us

You’ll find us in Tallinn’s modern WoHo district, where our custom-designed office makes it easy to feel at home. Each of our eight themed floors – park, nature, sports, IT, music, art, movies and culture – is fully immersive. Whether you’re jamming on the music floor’s instruments or exploring the art gallery, there’s something for everyone.

# Principal Product Manager

UK, London - CPTO - Hybrid

We’re looking for a London-based Principal Product Manager in an individual contributor role to own the Agentic AI experiences and the underlying AI platform capabilities that power them.

Apply now

Share this page

Back to all positions

We believe it takes great people to create a great product. That’s why our team lives our company values, and we hire based on them, too. Since 2010, Pipedrive has been on a mission to support sales and marketing teams with easy-to-use, powerful tools that make everyday work faster and easier. Today, our cloud-based software is trusted by over 100,000 companies and used in 179 countries. We have grown from a five-person team to a truly international company of over 850+ people, representing more than 50 nationalities, with ten offices distributed across Europe and the US. In 2020, Pipedrive received a majority investment from Vista Equity Partners, a global investment firm that invests exclusively in enterprise software, data and technology-enabled businesses, making Pipedrive the fifth unicorn from Estonia.

We’re looking for a Principal Product Manager in an individual contributor role to own the Agentic AI experiences and the underlying AI platform capabilities that power them. You will be responsible for defining how Agentic AI delivers value to our customers across the Pipedrive CRM, shaping experiences that are safe, reliable, and scalable. Additionally, you will build and contribute to the shared platform capabilities that enable product teams to deliver AI-powered experiences consistently and at scale.

The scope of this role spans both customer facing agentic AI capabilities and internal AI platform foundations. You will operate at a platform and systems level, working closely with engineering, applied AI and machine learning, design, security and product teams to ensure that agentic AI delivers meaningful customer value while remaining technically sound and operationally scalable.

This high-impact role reports to the VP of Product and is ideal for a senior product leader who combines strong product thinking and customer centricity with a deep understanding of AI systems and complex platforms.

title: New York | Jobs
content:
# New York

Welcome to the Big Apple! Our New York City office is located in the heart of Midtown Manhattan.

See open positions

## About us

Our first US office was in the Bay Area, but we moved to the East Coast in 2015 to be better connected to our European teams. Since then, New York has become the strategic hub for our US operations.

Today, you’ll find us in Midtown Manhattan with a close-knit team of over 15 Pipedrivers representing a range of departments. It’s a diverse and collaborative group, each person contributing their own perspective and experience.

The focus in New York leans more toward the business side – including sales, customer success, support and finance – and we all work together to keep things running smoothly and stay connected to our global mission.

## Our traditions

We believe strong teams are built both in and outside the office. From time to time, we take a break from our usual activities to share a meal, play a round of mini golf, see the New York Yankees play or volunteer together for causes we believe in.

One of our favorite annual highlights is Summer Garage – a company-wide event that brings Pipedrivers from around the world together for music, bonding and fun. And as the year wraps up, we celebrate during the winter holiday season with The Pipeys, our global award show where we recognize teammates who live our values every day.

No matter the event or season, we make time to enjoy each other’s company and create space for connection.

## Where you’ll find us

We’re located in the heart of Midtown Manhattan at 530 Fifth Avenue – just steps away from landmarks like Bryant Park, Times Square and Grand Central Station. The neighborhood is packed with great restaurants, culture and energy, offering something new to discover every day.

Our office is part of the Convene co-working space, which means we benefit from a range of shared amenities while enjoying the comfort of a private suite. The space accommodates up to 12 people and includes a meeting room and conference area. It may be compact, but there's plenty of space to work, collaborate and connect.

Whether you're grabbing lunch nearby or settling in with your laptop and coffee, you'll find everything you need to do your best work – right in the middle of it all.

## Meet our team

One of the things I value most about working at Pipedrive is the truly global collaboration. From my remote base in New York, I work closely with sales teams in Lisbon, London, Germany and Florida, and the connection between teams is seamless. The flexibility to work remotely while still having the option to spend time in the NYC office or visit colleagues in other hubs makes it a unique and rewarding place to grow. It’s the people and the supportive culture that make Pipedrive such a great place to work.

title: London | Jobs
content:
# London

Be a part of Pipedrive London’s growth story.

Join our dynamic team in this iconic city.

See open positions

## About us

Our London office is home to a fun and close-knit team of 30+ Pipedrivers. Over the years, we’ve tried it all, from co-working spaces and private offices to going fully remote. In March 2024, we found our new home in Holborn at Uncommon, right in the heart of London.

This small but mighty team represents almost every Pipedrive department (engineering, we’re saving a seat for you) and reflects the global nature of our company. We’re proud of the mix of nationalities, languages, skills and experiences that come together here.

Many of our executive team are also based in London, which means leadership is always close by for the moments that matter.

## Our traditions

Tea, biscuits and big ideas – that’s how many of our best conversations start. While coffee may be serious business at Pipedrive, London proudly leads with tea. You’ll also find cake on the table more often than not, from themed celebration bakes to treats during our weekly “Cake & Catch-up” or twice-monthly All Hands.

Our office culture blends energy and warmth. The buzz of our sales team provides its own soundtrack, and we keep things moving with gym classes, walk-and-talk meetings and the occasional lunchtime museum trip. We also love our rooftop terrace – perfect for summer drinks, team chats and soaking up the skyline.

Dogs are always welcome, and we’ve named our meeting rooms after our favorite local pubs, a small nod to British culture and a big part of what makes this space feel like home.

## Where you’ll find us

You’ll find us in Holborn at Uncommon, a creative and modern workspace in central London. From here, it’s easy to explore everything the city has to offer – Covent Garden, Soho and countless cafés, museums and parks are just steps away.

Our office space offers a balance of comfort and energy. The on-site café is ideal for grabbing coffee or lunch, and our rooftop terrace is a team favorite when the sun’s out. Inside, the space supports collaboration and focus – whether you’re deep in a strategy session or catching up with a teammate over tea.

There’s room here to do your best work and enjoy the people around you. Whether it’s brainstorming over biscuits, heading out for a walk or joining a spontaneous pub night, our London office makes work feel genuinely good.

## Meet our team

Pipedrive is the kind of place where you’re trusted to take ownership and shape things, not just follow a plan. You get a lot of room to work your own way, and people are always open to helping or hearing your ideas.

**Andy Mac**Senior Creative Designer, Video

Working at Pipedrive is quite frankly, amazing, in so, so many ways. A fantastic product, talented people and a chance to challenge yourself and your thinking every day. At the heart of it, Pipedrive truly cares about its people and believes there is a fun side to work, too.

**Elizabeth Martin**

Backbase has entered a new era of digital banking with the launch of the world’s first AI-Native Banking OS. This is not just a platform; it is a fundamental shift in how banks operate. By unifying digital channels, front-office workspaces, and back-office operations under a single Control Plane, we enable banks to run as one. Our OS coordinates execution across customers, employees, and AI agents, ensuring every action is authorized, governed, and context-aware. At Backbase, we are transforming the promise of AI into measurable business impact for over 120 leading financial institutions globally.

The Principal AI Engineer - Banking OS Control Plane for Integrations is a pivotal role within our Ecosystems department. In this role, you won't just be using tools to build connectors; you will be the core engineer responsible for building and enhancing the Integrations Control Plane - Connector Studio—our proprietary, AI-powered platform designed to enable the seamless creation of connectors.

You will architect the intelligent engine and the platform tools that empower teams to integrate diverse banking systems effortlessly. You are building the "brain" and the machinery of the Connectivity and Integration Layer (powered by Grand Central), which acts as the critical bridge between our intelligent frontline and the complex world of legacy core systems, CRMs, and fintechs.

We’re looking for a Principal AI Engineer with a passion and deep knowledge in building intelligent, context-aware agentic systems, tools, and platforms.
